Cushing's disease is caused by excessive corticotropin (ACTH) secretion by tumors in the pituitary gland, Cushing's syndrome results from sustained pathologic hypercortisolism caused by excessive corticotropin (ACTH) secretion by tumors in the pituitary gland or elsewhere, or by ACTH-independent cortisol secretion from adrenal tumors

Cushing's disease is a pituitary source of excess ACTH, Cushing's syndrome is endogenous hypercortisolism caused either by excess ACTH secretion or by autonomous cortisol release from the adrenal cortex
